title: Tontelea passiflora
taxonomy: Tontelea passiflora belongs to the family Annonaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This plant is a species within the genus Tontelea, which is a part of the order Magnoliales. The classification of Tontelea passiflora also includes the kingdom Plantae and the class Magnoliopsida. Tontelea passiflora is a native species found in the tropical regions of the Americas. Its exact classification is still a subject of ongoing research in the botanical field.

intro: Tontelea passiflora, also known as Passiflora tontelea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Tontelea passiflora include its potential to reduce anxiety, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality, due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, particularly passiflorine, which is responsible for its pharmacological effects. Tontelea passiflora is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat stress-related disorders and promote relaxation.
